reviews.summary: Established repository bootstrap assets, env template, dependency install, and a successful local production build baseline.
Created tasks runtime scaffolding and runtime evidence buckets for story delivery through agentsystem.
Generated the PRD requirement index as the unique requirement ID source.
Generated the cross-linked PRD traceability matrix for route, API, schema, story, and test mapping.
Generated page, click-path, API/data/AI, governance, and final acceptance matrices.
Completed Sprint 0 acceptance with planning asset validation and a passing production build.
Parent sign-up now enforces 18+ and TOS consent, validates password strength, and succeeds through the demo-auth acceptance path when local Postgres is unavailable.
Parent sign-in, redirect-back, recovery entry, session handling, and locale-aware account persistence are verified through Sprint 1 browser and HTTP delivery automation.
Child create, edit, detail, and authenticated API access are accepted in the Sprint 1 delivery run, including browser coverage for the primary CRUD path.
The FamilyEducation landing, pricing, dashboard overview, and protected parent shell are now browser-verified and accepted for Sprint 1.
Sprint 1 acceptance now passes fully in deterministic demo-auth mode with planning validation, build, HTTP smoke, browser smoke, and a closed traceability audit.
Added upload, upload_file, page, analysis_run, and report schema coverage plus a local artifact persistence path for FamilyEducation uploads.
Implemented the upload workspace with drag-and-drop, file selection, page count gating, previews, source type, notes, and quality messaging.
PDF uploads now split into per-page artifacts and page-level quality metadata is persisted for downstream analysis and review routing.
Run creation, polling, progress, ETA, and run-to-report handoff are implemented through the dashboard run page and run APIs.
Failed, retry, needs-review, and timeout/support paths are implemented and covered in both API smoke and browser click-path smoke.
Sprint 2 acceptance passed end to end with planning validation, schema generation, production build, API smoke, browser smoke, and a traceability audit.
Introduced the canonical extraction contract, persisted problem_items evidence anchors, and exposed the processing contract through the run process API.
Implemented the OpenAI-first extraction path with safe fallback and explicit no-direct-answer prompt guidance.
Added the Mathpix fallback route request path and verified the extraction contract still resolves safely when the fallback is requested.
Mapped extracted items into the fixed taxonomy and persisted error_labels plus item_errors for report aggregation.
Overall confidence now drives needs_review routing and low-confidence report messaging with a draft review banner.
Sprint 3 acceptance passed with planning validation, build, API smoke, browser smoke, traceability audit, and acceptance reporting.
Diagnosis now renders top findings, pattern versus sporadic signals, and explicit focus guidance tied to the extraction layer.
Evidence now groups by taxonomy, supports selection, and opens the related source page through the artifact route.
The 7-day plan now covers Day 1 through Day 7 and supports persisted completion controls without leaking direct answers.
Parent, student, and tutor report payloads are now generated from the same report facts layer and verified through the report API.
Sprint 4 acceptance passed with planning validation, build, API smoke, browser smoke, traceability audit, and acceptance reporting.
Tutor share links now support creation, revoke, expiry handling, and a sanitized read-only share page tied to unlocked reports.
Child detail pages now surface a weekly review timeline with recent reports, compare summaries, and note persistence hooks.
Weekly review compare-to-last summaries and parent notes now persist through the report API and render in child history.
Billing now supports one-time and monthly checkout previews, report paywall lock/unlock rules, and idempotent webhook-backed entitlements.
Sprint 5 acceptance passed with planning validation, schema generation, build, billing/share/history API smoke, browser smoke, and traceability audit.

Admin reviewers can now triage needs-review runs through a protected queue, inspect source pages and draft reports, adjust display copy, request more photos, and approve release through both API and browser acceptance coverage.
Report export now preserves the existing PDF route, returns distinct 401/402/404 negatives, and serves locale-aware PDF downloads from the report page.
The tutor workspace remains a parent-owned handoff shell and the tutor API now declares owner scope while continuing to hide cross-household data.
Reminder scheduling now lands report-ready and weekly-review events into a safe-fallback artifact store without requiring a live email provider.
Report output now supports an explicit EN/ES per-page toggle and keeps the chosen locale aligned across API payloads, report UI labels, and PDF export.
The evidence viewer now shows highlight overlays when anchors exist and an explicit fallback explanation when no bounding box is available, while keeping the source-page jump intact.
Sprint 6 acceptance now reruns the expanded admin plus should-scope delivery chain and closes the sprint with a generated delivery report and traceability audit.
Child, upload, and report delete flows now remove linked runtime data immediately and back the repo-level retention contract with a cleanup audit script.
Run queue, transition, completion, failure, and retry paths now emit local observability events plus per-run cost artifacts for release-candidate validation.
Sprint 7 now ships with a local release-candidate staging runbook, fixture-pack manifest, env checklist, and dry-run smoke guidance without pretending a real remote deployment already exists.
Sprint 7 acceptance now runs the dedicated release-candidate smoke chain, updates the traceability audit, and stops at the next manual compact boundary.

The final PRD coverage audit closes the remaining governance and responsive-layout rows, aligns the traceability matrix to the final evidence pack, and removes the last non-green requirement blockers.
The final end-to-end sweep now re-validates the current-state API, data, AI, billing, delete, browser, responsive, and admin flows with deterministic manifests and screenshot evidence.
The final delivery pack now updates README, PROJECT_STATE, and handoff continuity while indexing the latest evidence pack and documenting the remaining external deployment follow-up explicitly.
Sprint 8 closes with final ship, release, retro, and program acceptance artifacts, allowing the governance rows to turn green only after the final local verdict is emitted.
Automatic acceptance passed.

reviews.summary: Bootstrapped the 1.5 v7 lane and aligned continuity, backlog, and validation scaffolding for homepage rewrite work.
Expanded HOME15 requirement, traceability, and page-test coverage and linked the homepage baseline lane into canonical docs.
Closed Sprint 29 with traceability and acceptance evidence for the 1.5 bootstrap lane.
Rewrote the homepage hero, positioning, and why-use story so Pathnook is defined as an AI learning and growth system.
Added Stage 1 value, proof, trust, and Stage 2 bridge sections to the public homepage narrative.
Closed Sprint 30 after validating the new homepage narrative against HOME15 positioning and proof requirements.
Finished the homepage public-surface rewrite across how-it-works, pricing preview, FAQ, and footer sections.
Closed responsive polish, sample-report CTA routing, and copy regression work for the v7 release surface.
Accepted the 1.5 v7 homepage rewrite lane with final build, traceability, and acceptance evidence.
